GetPwrCapabilities.description: Uses thermal zones to check if hardware components exist; VMs typically report none.

SetupDiGetClassDevs.description: Enumerates device classes to retrieve hardware info; used to detect VM-specific HDD names.

SetupDiEnumDeviceInfo.description: Iterates over device info set entries to read HDD friendly names for VM detection.

SetupDiGetDeviceRegistryProperty.description: Reads device registry properties (SPDRP_FRIENDLYNAME) to identify VM-specific disk names.

DeviceIoControl.description: Sends IOCTLs to physical drives to check Vendor ID or disk size -- small disk is a VM indicator.

GetAdaptersAddresses.description: Enumerates network adapters to check MAC address OUI prefixes specific to VMs (VirtualBox: 08:00:27, VMware: 00:0C:29).

GetAdaptersInfo.description: Retrieves network adapter info to detect VM-specific MAC OUI prefixes or adapter name strings.

WNetGetProviderName.description: Queries network provider name; returns 'VirtualBox Shared Folders' in VirtualBox environments.

CreateToolhelp32Snapshot.description: Creates a process/module snapshot to search for VM-specific processes (vmtoolsd.exe, vboxservice.exe).

EnumProcesses.description: Enumerates all running process IDs to detect VM/sandbox-specific processes.

GetSystemFirmwareTable.description: Reads raw SMBIOS/ACPI/FIRM firmware tables to scan for VM artifact strings (VBOX, VMware, QEMU).

EnumSystemFirmwareTables.description: Enumerates firmware table providers as a precursor to reading SMBIOS/ACPI tables for VM artifacts.

NtQuerySystemInformation.description: Native NT API rarely called directly by legitimate code; used to detect boot time inconsistencies linked to VM snapshots.

Direct3DCreate9.description: Initializes Direct3D 9 to query physical display adapter properties via GetAdapterIdentifier.

MulDiv.description: Uses MulDiv API inconsistencies to detect Wine execution.

NtQueryLicenseValue.description: Queries the license value 'Kernel-VMDetection-Private'; returns 1 on Hyper-V environments including Windows Server VMs.

IsNativeVhdBoot.description: Checks if the OS was booted from a VHD file -- strong indicator of a sandbox or cloud-hosted analysis environment.

GetFileAttributesA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with FindFirstFileA for checking VM driver file existence.

GetFileAttributesW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with FindFirstFileW for checking VM driver file existence.

FindFirstFileA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetFileAttributesA for scanning VM artifact paths.

FindFirstFileW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetFileAttributesW for scanning VM artifact paths.

GetUserNameA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetComputerNameA -- used to compare against known sandbox usernames.

GetUserNameW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetComputerNameW -- used to compare against known sandbox usernames.

GetComputerNameA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetUserNameA -- used to compare against known sandbox hostnames.

GetComputerNameW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with GetUserNameW -- used to compare against known sandbox hostnames.

GetComputerNameExW.description: Native variant of GetComputerNameW; meaningful only alongside username or other environment checks.

GetDiskFreeSpaceExA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only alongside DeviceIoControl -- disk < 80 GB total or < 10 GB free signals a VM.

GetDiskFreeSpaceExW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only alongside DeviceIoControl -- disk < 80 GB total or < 10 GB free signals a VM.

GetVolumeInformationA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only with GetDiskFreeSpaceExA -- volume label 'VirtualBox Shared Folders' confirms VM.

GetVolumeInformationW.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only with GetDiskFreeSpaceExW -- volume label 'VirtualBox Shared Folders' confirms VM.

Process32First.description: Iterates over a Toolhelp32 snapshot; meaningful only in combination with CreateToolhelp32Snapshot and Process32Next.

Process32Next.description: Iterates over a Toolhelp32 snapshot; meaningful only in combination with CreateToolhelp32Snapshot and Process32First.

Process32FirstW.description: Unicode variant of Process32First; meaningful only alongside the full Toolhelp32 snapshot combo.

Process32NextW.description: Unicode variant of Process32Next; meaningful only alongside the full Toolhelp32 snapshot combo.

Module32First.description: Enumerates loaded modules via a Toolhelp32 snapshot; meaningful only combined with CreateToolhelp32Snapshot to detect sandbox DLLs.

Module32Next.description: Iterates loaded modules; meaningful only combined with CreateToolhelp32Snapshot and Module32First.

WaitForSingleObject.description: Common synchronization API; meaningful only with GetTickCount -- sandboxes accelerating time show an anomalously short delta.

WaitForMultipleObjects.description: Multi-object wait variant; meaningful only with GetTickCount for timing-based sandbox detection.

NtDelayExecution.description: Native sleep that bypasses hooks on Sleep() -- sandboxes that only hook the Win32 layer fail to accelerate this delay.

CreateWaitableTimer.description: Timer-based sleep alternative; meaningful only with GetTickCount to detect sandboxes that patch Sleep but not timer APIs.

timeSetEvent.description: Multimedia timer sleep alternative; meaningful only with GetTickCount for sleep-skipping sandbox detection.

IcmpSendEcho.description: ICMP-based timing delay; meaningful only with GetTickCount -- sandboxes not hooking ICMP reveal abnormal elapsed time.

OpenSCManagerA.description: Opens the service control manager; meaningful only combined with EnumServicesStatusW to enumerate VM-specific services.

OpenSCManagerW.description: Unicode variant of OpenSCManagerA; meaningful only combined with service enumeration for VM driver detection.

EnumServicesStatusW.description: Enumerates all running services; meaningful only combined with OpenSCManagerA/W to scan for VM driver services (VBoxService, vmtools).

CoInitializeEx.description: COM initialization required for WMI queries; meaningful only combined with CoCreateInstance for WMI-based VM detection.

CoCreateInstance.description: Instantiates IWbemLocator; meaningful only combined with CoInitializeEx -- pivot of all WMI-based VM/hardware queries.

RegOpenKeyEx.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegQueryValueEx or RegEnumKeyEx.

RegQueryValueEx.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegOpenKeyEx.

RegEnumKeyEx.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegOpenKeyEx.

GetModuleHandle.description: Ubiquitous; meaningful only in combination with GetProcAddress.

GetProcAddress.description: Ubiquitous; meaningful only in combination with GetModuleHandle.

CreateMutexA.description: Common synchronization primitive; meaningful only in combination with OpenMutexA.

OpenMutexA.description: Meaningful only in combination with CreateMutexA for VM/sandbox mutex name checks.

CreateFileA.description: Universal API; meaningful only in combination with DeviceIoControl for VM device path checks.

GetTickCount.description: Ubiquitous; meaningful only in combination with Sleep for timing-based sandbox detection.

Sleep.description: Ubiquitous; meaningful only in combination with GetTickCount for sleep-skipping detection.

QueryPerformanceCounter.description: Widely used for profiling; meaningful only in combination with QueryPerformanceFrequency.

QueryPerformanceFrequency.description: Widely used; meaningful only in combination with QueryPerformanceCounter for timing anomaly detection.

GetSystemInfo.description: Universal; meaningful only in combination with GlobalMemoryStatusEx for hardware fingerprinting.

GlobalMemoryStatusEx.description: Widely used; meaningful only in combination with GetSystemInfo for hardware fingerprinting.

GetSystemMetrics.description: Common in GUI apps; meaningful only in combination with GetCursorPos for headless environment detection.

GetCursorPos.description: Common in GUI apps; meaningful only in combination with GetSystemMetrics for headless detection.

GetLastInputInfo.description: Meaningful only in combination with GetSystemMetrics; absence of input = sandbox indicator.

FindWindowA.description: Common in GUI code; meaningful only in combination with EnumWindows for VM window detection.

EnumWindows.description: Meaningful only in combination with FindWindowA; low window count = sandbox indicator.

OpenProcess.description: Common; meaningful only in combination with CreateToolhelp32Snapshot for sandbox privilege detection.

EnumDisplayDevicesA.description: Enumerates display devices to read GPU name -- VM-specific strings (VMware SVGA, VirtualBox Graphics Adapter) confirm virtualized environment.

VMware, Inc..description: VMware vendor string -- registry or SMBIOS VM artifact.

VMwareVMware.description: VMware CPUID vendor string returned by leaf 0x40000000.

VBOX.description: VirtualBox artifact string found in binary.

VBoxVBoxVBox.description: VirtualBox CPUID vendor string returned by leaf 0x40000000.

VBoxGuest.description: VirtualBox guest driver registry key path.

VBoxService.description: VirtualBox service name used for process enumeration.

VBoxTrayToolWndClass.description: VirtualBox tray window class name used for FindWindowA-based detection.

vmtoolsd.exe.description: VMware Tools process name used for process enumeration.

vboxservice.exe.description: VirtualBox service process name used for process enumeration.

vmwaretray.exe.description: VMware tray process name used for process enumeration.

vmwareuser.exe.description: VMware user process; its presence confirms VMware Tools are installed.

vmacthlp.exe.description: VMware activation helper process; VM-specific process enumeration artifact.

vboxtray.exe.description: VirtualBox tray process name used for process enumeration.

VBoxControl.exe.description: VirtualBox guest control utility; VM-specific process artifact.

qemu-ga.exe.description: QEMU guest agent process; confirms QEMU/KVM environment.

prl_cc.exe.description: Parallels Control Center process; confirms Parallels Desktop environment.

sbiedll.dll.description: Sandboxie DLL name; checked via GetModuleHandle to detect sandboxed execution.

snxhk.dll.description: Sandboxie hook DLL; its presence in the loaded module list confirms Sandboxie instrumentation.

pstorec.dll.description: Fake pstorec DLL injected by Norman Sandbox for API monitoring.

dir_watch.dll.description: Anubis sandbox monitoring DLL; its presence confirms an Anubis analysis environment.

vmcheck.dll.description: VMware check DLL; presence indicates VM detection attempt.

api_log.dll.description: API logging DLL associated with sandbox instrumentation.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\VBoxMous.description: VirtualBox mouse driver file path; its existence confirms a VirtualBox guest environment.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\VBoxGues.description: VirtualBox guest driver file path; confirms VirtualBox Guest Additions are installed.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\VBoxSF.s.description: VirtualBox shared folders driver file path; VM-specific filesystem artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\VBoxVide.description: VirtualBox video driver file path; confirms VirtualBox virtualized display.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\vboxdisp.dll.description: VirtualBox display DLL; VM-specific user-mode driver artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\vboxhook.dll.description: VirtualBox hook DLL; VM-specific Windows hooks artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\vboxmrxnp.dll.description: VirtualBox network provider DLL; VM-specific shared folder artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\vmhgfs.s.description: VMware Host-Guest Filesystem driver; confirms VMware Tools installation.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\vmmouse..description: VMware mouse driver file path; VM-specific input device artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\vmmemctl.description: VMware memory balloon driver; VM-specific memory management artifact.

C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\vmci.sys.description: VMware VMCI driver file path; confirms VMware virtual machine communication interface.

VMware SVGA II.description: VMware virtual GPU name returned by EnumDisplayDevices or WMI Win32_VideoController.

VirtualBox Graphics Adapter.description: VirtualBox virtual GPU name returned by EnumDisplayDevices or WMI Win32_VideoController.

VBOX HARDDISK.description: VirtualBox virtual disk model string returned by SetupDi or WMI Win32_DiskDrive.

VMware Virtual disk.description: VMware virtual disk model string returned by SetupDi or WMI Win32_DiskDrive.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\VMware, Inc.\\VMware Too.description: VMware Tools registry key path used for registry-based VM detection.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Oracle\\VirtualBox Guest.description: VirtualBox Guest Additions registry key path.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Service.description: VirtualBox guest driver service registry path.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\ControlSet001\\Services\\V.description: VirtualBox shared folders service registry path.

HKLM\\HARDWARE\\ACPI\\DSDT\\VBOX__.description: VirtualBox ACPI DSDT table registry artifact; presence confirms VirtualBox virtualization.

HKLM\\HARDWARE\\ACPI\\FADT\\VBOX__.description: VirtualBox ACPI FADT table registry artifact; confirms VirtualBox environment.

HKLM\\HARDWARE\\ACPI\\RSDT\\VBOX__.description: VirtualBox ACPI RSDT table registry artifact; confirms VirtualBox environment.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Enum\\S.description: SCSI device enumeration registry path specific to VMware virtual disks.

\\\\.\\VBoxMiniRdrDN.description: VirtualBox device path used with CreateFileA for VM detection.

\\\\.\\pipe\\VBoxTrayIPC.description: VirtualBox tray IPC pipe path used with CreateFileA.

\\\\.\\VBoxGuest.description: VirtualBox guest device path; opening it confirms a VirtualBox environment.

\\\\.\\HGFS.description: VMware shared folders device path used with CreateFileA.

\\\\.\\vmci.description: VMware VMCI device path used with CreateFileA.

wine_get_unix_file_name.description: Wine-exclusive export; checked via GetProcAddress to detect Wine environment.

wine_get_host_version.description: Wine-exclusive export; checked via GetProcAddress to detect Wine environment.

Kernel-VMDetection-Private.description: License value queried via NtQueryLicenseValue; returns 1 on Hyper-V environments.

sandbox.description: Common sandbox username; compared against GetUserName result to detect automated analysis accounts.

malware.description: Common sandbox username; compared against GetUserName result to detect automated analysis accounts.

virus.description: Common sandbox username; compared against GetUserName result to detect automated analysis accounts.

SANDBOX.description: Common sandbox hostname; compared against GetComputerName result to detect automated analysis environments.

CUCKOO.description: Cuckoo sandbox hostname; its presence as a computer name confirms a Cuckoo analysis environment.

VIRTUAL HD.description: Virtual hard disk string found in device enumeration artifacts.

Hyper-V.description: Microsoft Hyper-V artifact string; may be legitimate in enterprise environments.

Microsoft Hv.description: Hyper-V CPUID vendor string returned by leaf 0x40000000.

prl hyperv.description: Parallels Desktop CPUID vendor string returned by leaf 0x40000000.

cpuid_check.description: CPUID instruction, used to detect hypervisor presence via ECX bit 31.

cpuid_hypervisor_leaf.description: MOV EAX, 0x40000000 followed by CPUID -- leaf 40000000h returns the hypervisor vendor string (VMwareVMware, VBoxVBoxVBox, KVMKVMKVM).

rdtsc_timing.description: RDTSC instruction, used for timing-based VM/sandbox detection.

vmware_io_port.description: MOV EDX, 0x5658 -- loads the VMware backdoor I/O port (VX) before an IN instruction to communicate with the VMware hypervisor.

sidt_check.description: SIDT [EBX] -- reads the IDT base register; on VMware the IDT pointer is relocated above 0xD0000000, exposing the hypervisor.

sgdt_check.description: SGDT [ESI] -- reads the GDT base register; relocate differs between bare metal and VM, used for the GDT trick.

sldt_check.description: SLDT EAX -- reads the LDT register; returns 0 on bare metal, non-zero on some hypervisors.

str_check.description: STR EAX -- Store Task Register; value differs between physical and virtualized environments.

combinations.name: Registry-based VM detection
combinations.description: Opens and queries registry keys -- likely reading VM-specific paths (VMware Tools, VBoxGuest, Hyper-V).

combinations.name: Registry enumeration VM detection
combinations.description: Opens and enumerates registry sub-keys -- likely scanning for VM-related service names.

combinations.name: Dynamic API resolution VM check
combinations.description: Dynamically resolves APIs at runtime -- likely checking for Wine-exclusive or sandbox-specific exports.

combinations.name: Mutex-based VM/sandbox detection
combinations.description: Creates and opens mutexes -- likely probing for VM/sandbox-specific mutex names.

combinations.name: Device path VM detection
combinations.description: Opens device paths and sends IOCTLs -- likely probing VM-specific paths (\\.\VBoxMiniRdrDN, \\.\vmci).

combinations.name: Sleep-skipping sandbox detection
combinations.description: Measures elapsed time around a Sleep call -- sandboxes that accelerate Sleep show an anomalously short delta.

combinations.name: High-resolution timing anomaly detection
combinations.description: Uses high-resolution timer to detect VM timing anomalies or RDTSC skew.

combinations.name: Hardware fingerprinting
combinations.description: Reads CPU count and total RAM -- low values (1 CPU, < 2 GB RAM) are classic VM/sandbox indicators.

combinations.name: Headless environment detection
combinations.description: Checks screen resolution and cursor position -- static cursor in low-res environment = headless sandbox.

combinations.name: User activity absence check
combinations.description: Combines input idle time with screen metrics -- no activity in low-resolution environment = sandbox.

combinations.name: Window enumeration sandbox detection
combinations.description: Searches for specific windows and enumerates all -- low count or VM class names indicate sandbox.

combinations.name: Process enumeration VM detection
combinations.description: Enumerates processes and attempts to open them -- likely scanning for VM/sandbox-specific process names.

combinations.name: Process name enumeration (Toolhelp32)
combinations.description: Full Toolhelp32 process iteration -- iterates all running processes to compare names against known VM/sandbox process list.

combinations.name: Module enumeration for sandbox DLLs
combinations.description: Iterates loaded modules -- likely scanning for sandbox-specific DLLs (sbiedll.dll, snxhk.dll, pstorec.dll).

combinations.name: Filesystem VM artifact check
combinations.description: Checks file existence and scans directories -- likely probing for VM driver files (VBoxMouse.sys, vmhgfs.sys, vmmouse.sys).

combinations.name: Alternate sleep timing detection
combinations.description: Measures elapsed time across a WaitForSingleObject call -- bypasses sandboxes that only hook Sleep().

combinations.name: Native sleep timing bypass
combinations.description: Uses native sleep to avoid Win32 Sleep() hooks, combined with system time queries to confirm sandbox time acceleration.

combinations.name: Comprehensive disk size check
combinations.description: Combines free space query with IOCTL disk size check -- disk < 80 GB or free space < 10 GB strongly indicates a VM or sandbox.

combinations.name: Service-based VM detection
combinations.description: Opens the service control manager and enumerates all services -- scanning for VM-specific services (VBoxService, VMTools, vmci).

combinations.name: WMI-based VM detection
combinations.description: Initializes COM and instantiates IWbemLocator -- required to issue WMI queries (Win32_ComputerSystem, Win32_BIOS, Win32_DiskDrive) for VM artifact scanning.

combinations.name: Sandbox environment check
combinations.description: Reads both username and hostname -- compares against known sandbox account names (sandbox, malware, virus) and hostnames (SANDBOX, CUCKOO).

combinations.name: Volume label VM detection
combinations.description: Queries volume name and disk size together -- 'VirtualBox Shared Folders' label combined with a small disk confirms a VirtualBox environment.
